problem_statement: Enterprise use of public LLMs risks inadvertent leakage of sensitive/IP source code, and existing security systems cannot distinguish business-critical code from generic code that share structural similarities; the paper builds a classifier to filter outgoing code before it reaches LLMs.
magnitude_evidence: On 10 days of real organizational traffic there were 'around 3442 incidents, out of which only 434 were the real incidents to check'
insufficiency_evidence: Existing security systems (DLP, access restrictions, monitoring) 'are not able to handle these variations'; and the model itself leaves 'There are 60 false negative incidents that could lead to data leaks without being noticed.'
notes: Tangential to the review's core: the mechanism prevents sensitive human-written code from being sent to LLMs (data-loss prevention), rather than overseeing AI-generated code output. Some references to unlabeled figures/tables (language distribution, confusion matrix).
